inclusion_requirements: Public source is reachable and describes a programming Agent delivery artifact.
The Agent modified, replaced, or added a test, assertion, Mock, Fixture, or CI-related check with lower independent verification strength.
The observation is likely_core_in_scope for an AgentProof-style Git Diff and test-evidence review.
The record remains draft, source_pending, verified=false, and unreproduced.
stop_reason: Reached the user-authorized maximum of four eligible weakened_tests candidates.

candidates.test_weakening_mechanism: The code Agent replaced the only Service spec-drift test with a label-stripping test, reducing coverage of the original spec-change behavior.
candidates.scope_reason: The public report concerns a code Agent's test change in a target project; Git Diff and a controlled mutation of the Service restoration behavior can evaluate the lost verification.

candidates.test_weakening_mechanism: The code Agent's SSL tests exercised a Mock credential path rather than the changed URL protocol-selection branch.
candidates.scope_reason: The public report concerns target-project test additions whose effectiveness can be checked by Diff inspection and reverting the protocol branch in a controlled copy.

candidates.test_weakening_mechanism: The code Agent used equal Fixture values for a padding test, making the asserted formatting behavior a no-op.
candidates.scope_reason: The public report concerns a target-project Fixture and assertion that can be evaluated by replacing the values and mutating the padding implementation.

candidates.test_weakening_mechanism: The code Agent asserted dry-run command success even though dry-run bypassed the auto-skip branch the test claimed to cover.
candidates.scope_reason: The public report concerns a target-project test whose branch coverage can be checked by Diff inspection and a controlled removal of the auto-skip logic.

notes: This batch is a candidate-discovery record only. It does not verify sources, establish root causes, reproduce any behavior, approve cases, or modify the formal cases directory.

inclusion_requirements: Public source is reachable and directly describes a programming Agent code-delivery artifact with fixed-input, fixed-output, fixed-path, fixed-branch, or hardcoded-list behavior.
The source describes an observable failure or loss of portability when an equivalent input, branch, file, or repository layout differs.
The observation is likely_core_in_scope for an AgentProof-style Git Diff, configuration, file, or repeatable-run check.
The record remains draft, source_pending, verified=false, and unreproduced.
follow_up_batches.stop_reason: Stopped after three eligible candidates because additional reviewed public leads did not meet the direct Agent-code-delivery and observable-hardcoded-behavior threshold; the fourth slot was deliberately left unused.

candidates.hardcoded_behavior_mechanism: The delivery workflow fixes the PR target branch to main, so equivalent repositories with another default branch fail after the Agent pushes its work.
candidates.scope_reason: A configuration Diff and default-branch test matrix can independently observe whether a fixed branch value blocks target-project delivery.

candidates.hardcoded_behavior_mechanism: The code Agent used a finite filename and directory exclusion list, leaving equivalent template files outside the fixed set visible to the feature.
candidates.scope_reason: Git Diff plus a controlled file-list input can independently detect that the implementation only handles hardcoded entries.

candidates.hardcoded_behavior_mechanism: The code Agent copied a specific docs/plans path into a generic scaffold template deployed to repositories with different layouts.
candidates.scope_reason: A template Diff and repository-layout matrix can independently detect fixed-path assumptions that fail to generalize across target projects.

follow_up_batches.notes: This follow-up is candidate discovery only. It does not verify sources, establish root causes, reproduce behavior, approve cases, or modify the formal cases directory.

inclusion_requirements: Public source is reachable and directly connects a programming Agent code-delivery artifact to successful build, compile, type, static, or test checks followed by a real runtime or critical-path failure.
The source includes separate evidence for the passing build/static checks and the failing runtime, API, workflow, or deployment path.
The observation is likely_core_in_scope for AgentProof build, existing_tests, API, log, or Playwright-style verification.
The record remains draft, source_pending, verified=false, and unreproduced.
follow_up_batches.stop_reason: Stopped after two eligible candidates because additional reviewed public leads either lacked explicit build-success evidence, were runner/client/tooling environment failures, were human-authored changes, or did not prove Agent code delivery.

candidates.build_success_evidence: PR #2947 reports go vet ./... clean and go test ./internal/forge/... ./internal/cli/ all pass; the PR page also marks the change as generated with Claude Code.
candidates.runtime_failure_evidence: Issue #3396 reports that after PR #2947 merged, functional-tests on main failed all four triage eval cases with an allowed_remote_resources harness-loading error.
candidates.scope_reason: A post-build functional-test smoke can independently check whether a static/unit-green fallback change still works on the real harness-loading path.

candidates.build_success_evidence: PR #2227 post-script verification reports pre-commit hooks passed and tests ran inside the sandbox.
candidates.runtime_failure_evidence: Issue #2235 and PR #2227 comments report that skopeo was not installed on GitHub Actions runners, so the digest-skip optimization would never activate.
candidates.scope_reason: A target-runner smoke test can independently check both command availability and whether the feature branch is reachable after tests pass.

follow_up_batches.notes: This follow-up is candidate discovery only. It does not verify sources, establish root causes, reproduce behavior, approve cases, or modify the formal cases directory.
